content.problemStatement:
Vietnamese and Bengali as well.

An NNN-bonacci sequence is an infinite sequence F1,F2,…F_1, F_2, \ldotsF1​,F2​,… such that for each integer i>Ni \gt Ni>N, FiF_iFi​ is calculated as f(Fi−1,Fi−2,…,Fi−N)f(F_{i-1}, F_{i-2}, \ldots, F_{i-N})f(Fi−1​,Fi−2​,…,Fi−N​), where fff is some function. A XOR NNN-bonacci sequence is an NNN-bonacci sequence for which f(Fi−1,Fi−2,…,Fi−N)=Fi−1⊕Fi−2⊕…⊕Fi−Nf(F_{i-1}, F_{i-2}, \ldots, F_{i-N}) = F_{i-1} \oplus F_{i−2} \oplus \ldots \oplus F_{i−N}f(Fi−1​,Fi−2​,…,Fi−N​)=Fi−1​⊕Fi−2​⊕…⊕Fi−N​, where ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes the bitwise XOR operation.

Recently, Chef has found an interesting sequence S1,S2,…S_1, S_2, \ldotsS1​,S2​,…, which is obtained from prefix XORs of a XOR NNN-bonacci sequence F1,F2,…F_1, F_2, \ldotsF1​,F2​,…. Formally, for each positive integer iii, Si=F1⊕F2⊕…⊕FiS_i = F_1 \oplus F_2 \oplus \ldots \oplus F_iSi​=F1​⊕F2​⊕…⊕Fi​. You are given the first NNN elements of the sequence FFF, which uniquely determine the entire sequence SSS.

You should answer QQQ queries. In each query, you are given an index kkk and you should calculate SkS_kSk​. It is guaranteed that in each query, SkS_kSk​ does not exceed 105010^{50}1050.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases ≤ 10^5
The sum of Q over all test cases ≤ 10^5
It is guaranteed that each answer fits in a 64‑bit signed integer.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with two space‑separated integers N and Q. The next line contains N space‑separated integers F1, F2, …, FN – the first N elements of the XOR‑N‑bonacci sequence. Then follow Q lines, each containing a single integer k, the index for which the prefix XOR S_k has to be reported.
content.outputFormat: For each query, output a single line containing the value S_k, the XOR of the first k elements of the underlying XOR‑N‑bonacci sequence.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=1 the sequence repeats every 2 positions. Prefix XORs: S0=0, S1=5. Hence odd k give 5, even k give 0.

solution.approach: For a XOR‑N‑bonacci sequence we have Fi = Fi‑1 ⊕ Fi‑2 ⊕ … ⊕ Fi‑N for i > N. Let Si = F1 ⊕ F2 ⊕ … ⊕ Fi be the prefix XOR. Using associativity of XOR we obtain Si = Si‑1 ⊕ Fi = Si‑1 ⊕ (Fi‑1 ⊕ … ⊕ Fi‑N) = Si‑(N+1). Hence the sequence of prefix XORs repeats with period N+1. Define S0 = 0 and for 1 ≤ i ≤ N, Si is the XOR of the first i given Fi. The answer for each query is pref[k % (N+1)] where pref[0…N] is pre‑computed in O(N).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N + Q) per test case. The total over all test cases is O(ΣN + ΣQ) ≤ 2·10^5.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) per test case for the prefix array.

code.Python3:
import sys
def main():
    data=list(map(int,sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()))
    it=iter(data)
    T=next(it)
    out=[]
    for _ in range(T):
        N=next(it);Q=next(it)
        pref=[0]*(N+1)
        for i in range(1,N+1):
            x=next(it)
            pref[i]=pref[i-1]^x
        for _ in range(Q):
            k=next(it)
            r=k%(N+1)
            out.append(str(pref[r]))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
if __name__=='__main__':
    main()
